docs.title: BigQuery ETL Skills
docs.text:
A comprehensive Claude Code plugin that accelerates development workflows on Mozilla's bigquery-etl repository using Claude Code Skills.

Designed for bigquery-etl

These skills work best when launched from your local development environment within the bigquery-etl repository.

docs.title: What are Skills?
docs.text:
Skills are folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that Claude loads dynamically to improve performance on specialized tasks. Skills teach Claude how to complete specific tasks in a repeatable way.

For more information, check out these docs from Antrhopic:

  • What are skills?
  • Using skills in Claude
  • How to create custom skills
  • Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills

docs.title: Using BigQuery ETL Skills
docs.text:
Once installed, Claude will automatically invoke the appropriate skills based on your requests. Just describe what you want to accomplish naturally.

  • Build new data models from scratch: From initial requirements gathering to production-ready queries, metadata, tests, and monitoring - skills work together to handle the complete workflow

  • Modify existing queries safely: Check downstream dependencies, gather requirements for changes, and update queries

  • Add comprehensive unit tests: Generate test fixtures with realistic input/output data for any query

  • Set up production monitoring: Create Bigeye data quality configurations

docs.text:

    Get up and running in minutes

  • Quick Start

    Jump right in with practical examples

docs.title: MkDocs Documentation Setup
docs.text: This document explains the MkDocs setup for BigQuery ETL Skills documentation.

docs.title: MkDocs Configuration
docs.text:
File: mkdocs.yml

  • Material theme with dark/light mode
  • Navigation structure for all skills and examples
  • Search functionality
  • Code highlighting
  • GitHub integration

docs.title: GitHub Actions Workflow
docs.text:
File: .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml

Automatically deploys documentation to GitHub Pages on push to main.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
File: requirements.txt

  • mkdocs
  • mkdocs-material
  • pymdown-extensions

docs.title: Install Dependencies
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Serve Documentation Locally
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Then visit: http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Build Documentation
docs.text:
mkdocs build

This creates a site/ directory with static HTML.

docs.title: Adding a New Page
docs.text:
1. Create the markdown file in docs/
  2. Add it to the nav in mkdocs.yml
  3. Test locally with mkdocs serve
  4. Commit and push

docs.title: Updating Existing Pages
docs.text:
1. Edit the markdown file
  2. Test locally
  3. Commit and push (auto-deploys)

docs.title: Updating Navigation
docs.text: Edit the nav: section in mkdocs.yml

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Use admonitions for notes, warnings, tips: 

    !!! note "Title"
    Content here

  • Add code blocks with syntax highlighting: 

    ```python
code here
```

  • Link between pages: 

    [Link text](../other-page.md)

  • Add images: 

    ![Alt text](../images/image.png)

docs.title: GitHub Pages URL
docs.text:
Once deployed, documentation will be available at:

https://mozilla.github.io/bigquery-etl-skills/

Temp link while private: https://friendly-adventure-1e683vz.pages.github.io/

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text:
• Keep documentation in sync with skill changes
  • Update examples when workflows change
  • Review and update periodically for accuracy
  • Monitor GitHub Actions for build failures

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Install the BigQuery ETL Skills plugin directly from a Claude Code session in your IDE

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: • Claude Code installed and configured

docs.title: 1. Launch Claude Code
docs.text:
Open your terminal and launch Claude Code:

claude

docs.title: 2. Add the Marketplace
docs.text:
Add this repository as a plugin marketplace in Claude Code:

/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/mozilla/bigquery-etl-skills.git

docs.title: 3. Install the Plugin
docs.text:
Install the bigquery-etl-skills plugin:

/plugin install bigquery-etl-skills

docs.title: 4. Verify Installation
docs.text:
The skills should now be available. Open a new Claude Code session and ask:

What skills are available?

You should see all 7 BigQuery ETL skills listed.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Once installed:

  • Check out the Quick Start guide for hands-on examples
  • Explore the Skills Overview for detailed documentation

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Skills Not Available?

If skills don't appear after installation, try:

  1. Restart Claude Code
  2. Open a new session
  3. Verify the marketplace was added: /plugin marketplace list
  4. Check plugin status: /plugin list

Working Directory

For best results with BigQuery ETL file generation, launch Claude Code from within your local bigquery-etl repository directory.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get hands-on with BigQuery ETL Skills through practical examples.

docs.title: Your First Query
docs.text:
Let's create a simple aggregation query from scratch:

Write a query that counts daily active users from clients_daily for Firefox Desktop

Claude will:

  1. ✅ Use bigquery-etl-core to understand conventions
  2. ✅ Use query-writer to create the SQL
  3. ✅ Use metadata-manager to generate schema and metadata files
  4. ✅ Optionally use sql-test-generator to create unit tests

docs.title: Create Unit Tests
docs.text: Create unit tests for telemetry_derived/events_daily_v1

docs.title: Update an Existing Query
docs.text:
Add a new field to telemetry_derived/clients_daily_v6
that calculates the ratio of searches with ads

docs.title: Gather Requirements
docs.text:
I need to create a table tracking campaign click-through rates.
Help me gather requirements.

docs.title: Add Monitoring
docs.text: Add Bigeye monitoring for telemetry_derived/clients_daily_v6

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Automatic Skill Selection

Claude automatically selects the right skills based on your request. You don't need to explicitly invoke them—just describe what you want naturally!

When you make a request, Claude:

  1. Analyzes your intent
  2. Selects appropriate skills
  3. Executes the workflow
  4. Iterates based on your feedback

docs.title: Tips for Success
docs.text:
Be Specific

  • ✅ "Create a daily aggregation query for active users"
  • ❌ "Help with a query"

Include Paths

Mention specific tables: telemetry_derived/clients_daily_v6

Work in Context

Launch Claude from your bigquery-etl directory for file operations

docs.title: Available Skills
docs.text: This plugin provides 7 specialized skills for BigQuery ETL workflows. Skills are automatically invoked by Claude based on your requests - you don't need to call them explicitly.

docs.title: Foundation
docs.text: Skill Purpose Claude uses when bigquery-etl-core Project conventions and patterns Checking naming conventions, understanding project structure

docs.title: Construction Workflows
docs.text: Skill Purpose Claude uses when model-requirements Requirements gathering Planning new tables or understanding existing ones query-writer SQL/Python query writing Creating or updating query.sql, query.py files or ad-hoc queries metadata-manager Schema and metadata config Generating schema.yaml & metadata.yaml files with descriptions sql-test-generator Unit test generation Creating, updating, and running unit tests bigconfig-generator Data quality monitoring Adding Bigeye monitoring configurations

docs.title: Meta Skills
docs.text: Skill Purpose Claude uses when skill-creator Create new skills Extending the plugin with new capabilities

docs.title: How Skills Work Together
docs.text: Skills are designed to be composable—they work together to complete complex workflows:

docs.title: Example: Creating a New Table
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Request] --> B[model-requirements]
    B --> C[query-writer]
    C --> D[metadata-manager]
    D --> E[sql-test-generator]
    E --> F[bigconfig-generator]
    F --> G[Complete Table]
  1. model-requirements gathers requirements
  2. query-writer creates the SQL
  3. metadata-manager generates schema/metadata
  4. sql-test-generator creates tests
  5. bigconfig-generator adds monitoring

docs.title: Example: Updating a Query
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Request] --> B[query-writer]
    B --> C[metadata-manager]
    C --> D[sql-test-generator]
    D --> E[Updated Query]
  1. query-writer updates the query
  2. metadata-manager updates the schema
  3. sql-test-generator updates tests

See the Skills Overview for detailed information about skill anatomy and coordination.

docs.title: Skills Overview
docs.text: The BigQuery ETL Skills plugin includes 7 specialized skills that work together to accelerate your workflows.

docs.title: Anatomy of a Skill
docs.text:
Each skill is a folder containing instructions, templates, and reference materials that Claude loads on-demand:

skill-name/
├── SKILL.md                    # Main skill instructions
├── assets/                     # Templates and examples
│   ├── template1.sql
│   ├── example1.py
│   └── ...
├── references/                 # Detailed reference docs
│   ├── conventions.md
│   ├── patterns.md
│   └── ...
└── scripts/                    # Helper scripts (optional)

docs.title: SKILL.md
docs.text:
The main skill file with three key parts:

1. Frontmatter Metadata 

---
name: skill-name
description: When Claude should invoke this skill
---

The description field is critical—it tells Claude when to automatically invoke the skill based on user requests.

2. Instructions

Step-by-step guidance for completing the skill's task:

  • What to read first (references, templates)
  • How to approach the work
  • What to generate or update
  • Required validations
  • When to invoke other skills

3. Coordination Points

Explicit instructions for working with other skills: 

## Skill Coordination
- After completing X, invoke metadata-manager
- If Y is needed, invoke sql-test-generator

docs.title: assets/
docs.text:
Templates and examples that Claude can read and copy:

  • SQL query templates
  • Python script templates
  • Configuration examples
  • Sample input/output data

These files provide concrete patterns to follow, reducing hallucination and ensuring consistency.

docs.title: references/
docs.text:
Detailed documentation broken into focused topics:

  • Conventions and standards
  • Common patterns and anti-patterns
  • External documentation links
  • Decision trees and workflows

Breaking knowledge into smaller files helps Claude load only what's relevant for each task.

docs.title: scripts/ (optional)
docs.text: Helper scripts for validation, generation, or analysis—used when bash operations are needed during skill execution.
